comments.body: STATUS 2026-07-12 (/rebar-implement): IMPLEMENTED + COMMITTED locally as 571d2cf37 on branch epic/9d50-calibration-followup (worktree /Users/joeoakhart/rebar-epic-9d50), part of an 8-commit stack. Locally green: pre-commit make lint + make typecheck passed; code leaves validated under held-out TDD (RED-first, implementer held out from the edge oracle, restored full oracle green, mutation/teeth check). NOT yet pushed to Gerrit and NOT closed. REMAINING: push the stack to gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, get LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1, then transition b9c0-2b03-36de-469f in_progress closed (completion-verifier close gate runs). Do NOT close before this change's Gerrit Verified +1 (per CLAUDE.local.md).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
With the threshold empirically derived (sibling story), flip the serious-tier code-review criteria from advisory-in-everything-but-name to actually able to BLOCK. Today only the two DET criteria (secret-detection, high-critical-security) can block; all 13 non-DET LLM criteria ship blocking_enabled: false at 0.95 — locked as the permissive-rollout invariant by ADR 0036 and pinned by tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py::test_code_review_only_det_security_criteria_are_hard_blocks. The field false-block risk is demonstrably near zero (0 finding-blocks in 162 changes).

- CRITICAL DISTINCTION (the reviewer's catch): `blocking_enabled` lives on CRITERIA (the routing keys in criteria_routing.json: `security`, `api-compat`, `db-migrations`, `deletion-impact`, `tests`, `scope-intent`, …) — NOT on the consequence-BINARIES (`data_loss_without_recovery`, etc.), which are decide.py severity attributes that set a finding's IMPACT, not routing entries. So the flip is: turn `blocking_enabled: true` on the CRITERIA whose Pass-1 findings carry the serious (0.9) binaries.

## What
- DEFINITE MINIMAL SET (not a provisional mapping): consequence-binaries are scored per-finding by the verify pass (content-based), NOT "owned" by a criterion, so there is no principled binary→criterion table to write. The empirical basis for WHICH criteria are block-worthy is sibling 9f25's adjudication. Therefore this ticket flips exactly ONE criterion now: set `blocking_enabled: true` + `block_threshold` = the EXACT numeric value 9f25 records as its derived priority-crossover threshold (b9c0 is stacked ABOVE 9f25, so that number exists at implementation time; b9c0 copies it verbatim — no independent guess) on **`security`** (the AGENT criterion under which the #518-class importlib code-execution finding lands — a security finding). Every other LLM criterion stays advisory at 0.95; the two DET criteria (secret-detection, high-critical-security) stay blocking as-is.
- Any EXPANSION of the blocking set beyond `security` (e.g. api-compat, db-migrations, deletion-impact, tests) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE here and gated on 9f25's per-criterion adjudication showing those criteria's findings are block-worthy at the derived threshold — filed as a follow-up if 9f25's data supports it, NOT guessed here. (This is why #539-class correctness findings — which have no clean owning criterion in the overlay set — are not force-mapped.)
- Update ADR 0036 (dated note: permissive rollout concluded for `security`, evidence attached) and ALL THREE blocking-set pinning tests, each updated to the new approved set `{secret-detection, high-critical-security, security}` and each still asserting NO OTHER criterion blocks: `tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py::test_code_review_only_det_security_criteria_are_hard_blocks`, `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py` — ALL THREE test functions that pin `security` as advisory/non-blocking break when `security` flips and must be updated: `test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095` (L39 `reg.threshold_for(["security"]) == (0.95, False)`), `test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking` (L52 `reg.threshold_for(["security","performance"]) == (0.95, False)`), and `test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff` (L64 `set(blocking)`), AND `tests/unit/test_code_review_deletion_impact.py` (lines ~102/104 pin `deletion-impact` blocking_enabled is False — which stays False under this minimal set, so that test's assertion is UNCHANGED but must be confirmed to still hold).
- Keep pass-3 separation intact: this is a threshold/posture CONFIG change only — no decide.py math changes.
- NOTE: this depends on 9f25's derived threshold; if 9f25's honest crossover comes out ABOVE 0.54/0.60, AC4's "#518-class findings now BLOCK" is revisited with 9f25's actual number (do not force a threshold to satisfy the AC — the epic's Definitions record this contingency).

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json, tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py, tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py + tests/unit/test_code_review_deletion_impact.py (the second and third blocking-set pinning tests), docs/adr/0036-*.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md (the cross-gate posture note), tests/unit/test_code_review_blocking_replay.py (self-contained serious-vs-NIT blocking replay). Depends on the derivation story 9f25 (consumes its derived threshold + its code-review derivation/replay script).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The `security` criterion has blocking_enabled: true at the derived block_threshold in criteria_routing.json; all other LLM criteria remain advisory at 0.95 (expansion beyond `security` is deferred to 9f25's data).
- [ ] ALL THREE blocking-set pinning tests assert the new approved set `{secret-detection, high-critical-security, security}` and each fails on any unapproved addition: test_impact_model_versioning.py::test_code_review_only_det_security_criteria_are_hard_blocks, test_code_review_ws2.py (all three security-pinning functions: test_threshold_for_default_overlay_is_advisory_at_095, test_threshold_for_takes_min_threshold_and_any_blocking, test_secrets_security_keys_are_the_ws5_blocking_handoff), and test_code_review_deletion_impact.py (confirm its `deletion-impact blocking_enabled is False` assertion still holds under the minimal set).
- [ ] ADR 0036 carries a dated enablement note citing derivation ticket 9f25.
- [ ] A SELF-CONTAINED replay proves #518-class (security) findings now BLOCK under the now-blocking `security` criterion while false-blocks stay 0 — WITHOUT depending on any sibling's not-yet-built script. Proving artifact (owned by this ticket): a unit test `tests/unit/test_code_review_blocking_replay.py` that (a) constructs a handful of finding decision-inputs — the per-finding {criterion:'security', validity, severity_attributes/impact} dicts that `pass3_decide`/`reg.threshold_for` actually consume (NOT the code_review_result_v1 sidecar bucket JSON, which is the persistence shape, not the decision input) — modelling a couple of #518-class (importlib code-execution) security findings at their real priority plus a set of low-priority NIT findings, and (b) runs them through `reg.threshold_for(['security'])` + the priority≥threshold block decision with the flipped config, asserting the security findings block (priority ≥ derived threshold, blocking=True) while the NIT set yields 0 blocks. Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_blocking_replay.py -q`. (9f25 owns the corpus-wide adjudication + derivation; this AC only proves the config change makes qualifying findings block.) If 9f25's honest threshold cannot make these findings block without false-blocks, this AC is renegotiated against 9f25's data rather than forced.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.

file_impact.reason: flip blocking_enabled=true + derived threshold on the security criterion only
confirm deletion-impact stays non-blocking under minimal set
self-contained replay: #518-class security finding blocks, NIT set 0
dated enablement note for security, citing 9f25

compiled_state.title: Enable the code-review blocking lane for serious-tier criteria at the derived threshold
